classification.statement: This appears to be a lease agreement.

summary: This document is a rental lease. It covers your rent, deposit, lease term, fees, and responsibilities, and what happens if the lease ends early.

what_this_means.text: This appears to be a lease agreement. It mentions 7 amount(s) of money. 7 clause(s) are worth reading carefully before you sign or respond. A professional can confirm anything that affects an important decision.
what_this_means.tone_check: Written in plain English without professional advice.

risks.title: Automatic Renewal
risks.explanation: This document appears to renew automatically unless you cancel before a specific date.
risks.why_it_matters: You could be charged again or locked in for another term if you miss the cancellation window.
risks.question_to_ask: What is the exact deadline to cancel before renewal?

risks.explanation: Ending this agreement before its end date appears to carry a cost or penalty.
risks.why_it_matters: Leaving early could be expensive; the amount and conditions matter.
risks.question_to_ask: What exactly would I owe if I ended this agreement early?

risks.title: Arbitration Clause
risks.explanation: Disputes appear to go to arbitration instead of court.
risks.why_it_matters: This can limit how you resolve disagreements, including giving up a jury trial or class action.
risks.question_to_ask: Can I opt out of the arbitration clause, and how?

risks.why_it_matters: Waivers can be broad; it's worth knowing exactly what you would be giving up.

actions.action: Read the 'Automatic Renewal' clause carefully and ask about it before agreeing.
actions.reason: You could be charged again or locked in for another term if you miss the cancellation window.

actions.action: Read the 'Early Termination Fee' clause carefully and ask about it before agreeing.
actions.reason: Leaving early could be expensive; the amount and conditions matter.

actions.action: Read the 'Arbitration Clause' clause carefully and ask about it before agreeing.
actions.reason: This can limit how you resolve disagreements, including giving up a jury trial or class action.

actions.action: Read the 'Waiver of Rights' clause carefully and ask about it before agreeing.
actions.reason: Waivers can be broad; it's worth knowing exactly what you would be giving up.
